compiled_state.title: As the doc-writer agent, I write dev-team documentation to the correct project-local directories
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
Scope: Update plugins/dso/agents/doc-writer.md to:
1. Add docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/archive/ to the 4-tier schema using positive enumeration framing
2. Add a NEVER-statement prohibiting writing to plugins/dso/
3. Move the plugin boundary constraint to the Role/Constraints section (top of file, before any procedure)

The constraint placement at the top of the file is required to guard against positional bias — agents in long-context sessions weight constraints encountered early more heavily than those encountered in later procedure sections.

The 4-tier schema update: Add these directories as a new entry (e.g., under Tier 2 user-facing or as a separate dev-team artifacts tier). The schema must positively enumerate: docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/archive/ with descriptions of what belongs in each.

Terminology must match the CLAUDE.md plugin boundary rule (S3) exactly — same directory names, same NEVER phrasing.

Done Definitions:
- [ ] plugins/dso/agents/doc-writer.md Role/Constraints section (at or near top of file) contains: list of permitted project-local artifact directories (docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/archive/) AND NEVER write to plugins/dso/
- [ ] Plugin boundary constraint appears before Section 1 (The Bright Line Decision Engine) in the file
- [ ] The 4-tier schema section names docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/archive/ as valid output locations with descriptions
- [ ] Terminology matches S3 CLAUDE.md rule exactly — run diff check before closing
- [ ] A behavioral test in tests/ verifies the routing rule: given a design artifact request, the agent prompt routes to docs/designs/ not plugins/dso/docs/designs/

Considerations:
- [Testing] Behavioral test must check routing decision (destination path), not file content — assert the path the agent is directed to write to, not what it writes
- [Testing] Use the behavioral testing standard (plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/behavioral-testing-standard.md) — test observable behavior, not implementation details
- [Maintainability] Coordinate terminology with S3 before closing — both stories touch the same conceptual constraint in different files

Escalation Policy: Escalate when blocked — proceed unless a significant assumption could send implementation in the wrong direction; document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: REPLAN_ESCALATE: All done definitions for this story (DD1, DD2, DD5) require hardcoding the string 'plugins/dso/' inside doc-writer.md and test-plugin-boundary-rule.sh. doc-writer.md is a distributed plugin agent file — every host project that installs DSO receives it. Hardcoding the host-project install path 'plugins/dso/' in a distributed artifact is architecturally impossible to satisfy correctly. Reverted commit 3eacc31c which added a test assertion expecting this constraint in doc-writer.md. The story as written cannot be implemented without violating the plugin boundary it is trying to enforce. Needs brainstorm re-examination: the correct enforcement mechanism for distributed plugin files must use host-agnostic language (e.g., the plugin install root as a variable, or a convention such as 'this plugin directory') rather than a hardcoded host-project path.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
REPLAN: Done definitions revised per corrected epic spec (brainstorm re-run 2026-04-10). The original DDs required a NEVER prohibition referencing plugins/dso/ inside doc-writer.md — a category error since doc-writer.md is a distributed plugin file.

Revised done definitions:
- DD1: plugins/dso/agents/doc-writer.md contains an Output Scope section placed immediately after the opening role statement, before the Input Requirements section
- DD2: The Output Scope section uses host-agnostic positive enumeration: docs/designs/ (design and UI artifacts), docs/findings/ (investigation results), docs/archive/ (superseded documents)
- DD3: The Output Scope section includes a halt condition — if the resolved output path is not under one of the four Documentation Schema tiers or these three artifact directories, the agent stops and reports the intended path to the orchestrator before writing
- DD4: No prohibition in doc-writer.md references the plugin install path by name (no hardcoded plugins/dso/ prohibition)
- DD5: A test verifies the Output Scope section is present before Input Requirements and enumerates docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/archive/
comments.author: Test
